symbol_map.description: Post-treatment dose amount, constant within unit across periods and zeroed out for never-treated units.
First-difference outcome Y_post - Y_pre used as the basic regression target.
PT-identified local level-effect curve evaluated at the realized dose group.
SPT-identified global level-effect curve among treated units.
SPT-identified marginal response curve, implemented as a derivative or adjacent-dose slope.
Cubic B-spline basis evaluated at dose d for ATT(d) estimation.
Derivative of the spline basis used to map the fitted curve into ACRT(d).
Data-driven CCK sieve dimension chosen before constructing the spline basis and confidence bands.

fidelity_rules.name: target_parameter
fidelity_rules.implementation_note: level targets ATT-family level effects; slope targets ACRT-family causal responses and requires stronger interpretation care under strong parallel trends.

fidelity_rules.name: aggregation
fidelity_rules.implementation_note: dose collapses across timing groups onto the dose axis; eventstudy collapses across doses onto exposure length and remains the preferred parallel-trends diagnostic surface.

fidelity_rules.name: dose_est_method
fidelity_rules.implementation_note: parametric uses a user-specified spline/polynomial parity path; cck uses the adaptive sieve estimator and is currently restricted to two-period, non-staggered dose aggregation.

fidelity_rules.name: control_group
fidelity_rules.implementation_note: the paper derivations use never-treated notation, but the R implementation also exposes not-yet-treated and eventually-treated comparison sets for staggered designs; fidelity code must preserve the chosen comparison semantics.

public_api_boundary.boundary_note: Public Python APIs may stay Pythonic, but they must preserve traceability back to the paper/R runtime enums recorded above.
